name: Poisons
description: Rules for poison types, acquisition, and 14 example poisons used as tools by assassins and evil creatures
introduction: Given their insidious and deadly nature, poisons are a favorite tool among assassins and evil creatures.

poison_types.title: Poison Types
poison_types.description: Poisons come in the following four types

contact.name: Contact
contact.description: Contact poison can be smeared on an object and remains potent until it is touched or washed off
contact.mechanics: A creature that touches contact poison with exposed skin suffers its effects

ingested.name: Ingested
ingested.description: A creature must swallow an entire dose of ingested poison to suffer its effects
ingested.delivery: The dose can be delivered in food or a liquid
ingested.dm_note: You may decide that a partial dose has a reduced effect, such as allowing Advantage on the saving throw or dealing only half as much damage on a failed save

inhaled.name: Inhaled
inhaled.description: Poisonous powders and gases take effect when inhaled
mechanics: Blowing the powder or releasing the gas subjects creatures in a 5-foot Cube to its effect
The resulting cloud dissipates immediately afterward
Holding one's breath is ineffective against inhaled poisons, as they affect nasal membranes, tear ducts, and other parts of the body

injury.name: Injury
injury.description: Injury poison can be applied as a Bonus Action to a weapon, a piece of ammunition, or similar object
injury.mechanics: The poison remains potent until delivered through a wound or washed off
injury.effect: A creature that takes Piercing or Slashing damage from an object coated with the poison is exposed to its effects

purchasing_poison.title: Purchasing Poison
purchasing_poison.overview: In some settings, laws prohibit the possession and use of poison, but an illicit dealer or unscrupulous apothecary might keep a hidden stash
acquisition: Characters with criminal contacts might be able to acquire poison easily
Other characters might have to make extensive inquiries and pay bribes before they acquire the poison they seek

harvesting_poison.title: Harvesting Poison
harvesting_poison.description: How to extract poison from venomous creatures
harvesting_poison.requirements: A character can attempt to harvest poison from a venomous creature that is dead or has the Incapacitated condition

results.success: The character harvests enough poison for a single dose, and no additional poison can be harvested from that creature
results.failure: The character is unable to extract any poison
results.critical_failure: If the character fails the check by 5 or more, the character is subjected to the creature's poison

sample_poisons.title: Sample Poisons
sample_poisons.description: Example poisons are detailed here in alphabetical order
sample_poisons.note: Each poison's description includes the suggested price for a single dose, its type, and a description of the poison's debilitating effects

failure.effect: Has the Poisoned condition for 24 hours

mechanics.success: Takes half as much damage only

failure.recurring: Must repeat the save at the start of each of its turns

mechanics.successive_failures: Takes 3 (1d6) Poison damage on each successive failed save
mechanics.cure: After three successful saves, the poison ends

failure.effect: Has the Poisoned condition for 1 minute
failure.additional: Also has the Paralyzed condition while Poisoned in this way

mechanics.recovery: The creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success

failure.effect: Has the Poisoned condition for 8 hours
failure.additional: Also has the Unconscious condition while Poisoned in this way

mechanics.waking: The creature wakes up if it takes damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake

failure.effect: Has the Poisoned condition for 1 hour

critical_failure.trigger: If the creature fails the save by 5 or more
critical_failure.effect: Also has the Unconscious condition while Poisoned in this way

mechanics.waking: The creature wakes up if it takes damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake

failure.effect: Has the Poisoned condition for 1 hour
failure.additional: Also has the Blinded condition while Poisoned in this way

mechanics.onset: A creature that ingests Midnight Tears suffers no effect until the stroke of midnight
mechanics.neutralization: Any effect that ends the Poisoned condition neutralizes this poison
mechanics.trigger: If the poison hasn't been neutralized before midnight
mechanics.damage: 31 (9d6) Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one

failure.effect: Has the Poisoned condition for 24 hours
failure.additional: Also has the Unconscious condition while Poisoned in this way

mechanics.waking: It wakes up if it takes damage

recurring.damage: 3 (1d6) Poison damage on a failed save

mechanics.special: The damage the poison deals can't be healed by any means while the creature remains Poisoned
mechanics.cure: After seven successful saves against the poison, the creature is no longer Poisoned

mechanics.damage: 35 (10d6) Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one
10 (3d6) Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one

failure.effect: Has the Poisoned condition for 4d6 hours
failure.additional: Speed is halved while the creature is Poisoned in this way

failure.effect: Has the Poisoned condition for 1 hour
failure.special: The Poisoned creature can't knowingly communicate a lie

mechanics.damage: 24 (7d6) Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one

contact: Carrion Crawler Mucus (200 gp, DC 13, Poisoned + Paralyzed 1 min)
Oil of Taggit (400 gp, DC 13, Poisoned + Unconscious 24 hrs)
ingested: Assassin's Blood (150 gp, DC 10, 1d12 Poison + Poisoned 24 hrs)
Midnight Tears (1,500 gp, DC 17 at midnight, 9d6 Poison)
Pale Tincture (250 gp, DC 16, 1d6 Poison + Poisoned daily checks, 7 saves)
Torpor (600 gp, DC 15, Poisoned 4d6 hrs, half Speed)
Truth Serum (150 gp, DC 11, Poisoned 1 hr, can't lie)
inhaled: Burnt Othur Fumes (500 gp, DC 13, 3d6 Poison, recurring saves, 3 successes to end)
Essence of Ether (300 gp, DC 15, Poisoned 8 hrs + Unconscious)
Malice (250 gp, DC 15, Poisoned 1 hr + Blinded)
injury: Lolth's Sting (200 gp, DC 13, Poisoned 1 hr, DC 13-5 for Unconscious)
Purple Worm Poison (2,000 gp, DC 21, 10d6 Poison)

contains: Four poison types (Contact, Ingested, Inhaled, Injury)
Purchasing poison rules and acquisition methods
Harvesting poison from venomous creatures (DC 20 Nature check)
14 example poisons with mechanics and prices
Poison effects including damage, conditions, and durations
Poison organization by type and price point
Special mechanics (delayed onset, healing immunity, daily recurring saves)
